obsidian.$description: Obsidian — Page canvas, nav background, poster grid ground

bone.$description: Bone — All text, hairline section borders, image borders, icon strokes

romieligatures-regular.$description: Display headlines, section titles, marquee text. The signature choice: this didone carries discretionary ligatures (dlig) that fuse letters into ornamental shapes — the oversized wordmarks and repeated marquee bands rely on these ligatures for their distinctive character. Line-height collapses to 0.73 at the 167px size so stacked display lines almost touch, creating a dense editorial block. Reserved exclusively for type-as-art moments; never used for body copy or UI labels.

lifeltstd-roman.$description: Navigation links, metadata, body copy, timestamps, section category labels. A single weight at a single size — the system does not modulate emphasis through weight. Tracking is normal. This is the functional typeface that scaffolds the UI while the display face performs. Its quietness is the point: LifeLTStd is a transitional serif that reads cleanly at 18px without competing with the display type.

obsidian-canvas.$description: Surface level 1: Full-bleed page background, navigation bar

bone-image-frame.$description: Surface level 2: Hairline borders around poster thumbnails and between sections — the only non-text white usage
